theory.intro: Two simple summaries describe a data display: the **range** (how spread out the values are) and the **mode** (the value that appears most often).
facts: **Mode** is the value with the most dots, or the tallest column.
A data set can have **no mode** (all equal) or more than one mode.
steps: Find the smallest and largest values and subtract to get the range.
Find the value that occurs most often — that is the mode.
To compare two displays, work out each one's range and mode.
